msg: warning: Variable fast_no_hz is used uninitialized!
reasoning: The variable 'char fast_no_hz[72]' is uninitialized unless CONFIG_RCU_FAST_NO_HZ is defined, in which case print_cpu_stall_fast_no_hz writes to it via sprintf. When !CONFIG_RCU_FAST_NO_HZ, the function body is empty and fast_no_hz remains uninitialized before printf at line 23 — a real bug confirmed in vbdb (1c17e4d) as UninitializedVariable with config 'RCU_CPU_STALL_INFO && !RCU_FAST_NO_HZ'. The tool reports only 'def(CONFIG_RCU_CPU_STALL_INFO)' as the presence condition, but this is over-constrained: in configurations where both CONFIG_RCU_CPU_STALL_INFO and CONFIG_RCU_FAST_NO_HZ are defined, fast_no_hz IS initialized and there is no bug. The condition should be def(CONFIG_RCU_CPU_STALL_INFO) && !def(CONFIG_RCU_FAST_NO_HZ).
